generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance HeatbedActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Primary actuator for temperature regulation in styrofoam incubator. Controls heating power (on/off state) with bang-bang control logic."
]

instance FanActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Distributes heat uniformly inside styrofoam box with variable speed control via controller commands."
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance InternalTemperatureSensor1 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Temperature sensor inside styrofoam box (first internal sensor) for continuous monitoring of internal thermal state."
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureDataStream
]

instance InternalTemperatureSensor2 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Temperature sensor inside styrofoam box (second internal sensor) for continuous monitoring of internal thermal state."
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureDataStream
]

instance ExternalTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Temperature sensor outside styrofoam box for monitoring ambient environmental conditions."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ExternalTemperatureDataStream
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance InternalTemperatureDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor1, InternalTemperatureSensor2
]

instance ExternalTemperatureDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ExternalTemperatureSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance SimplifiedThermalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simplified 2-parameter thermal model predicting temperature dynamics inside incubator (generalized thermal behavior)."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RabbitMQServer
]

instance EnhancedThermalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Enhanced 4-parameter thermal model predicting heatbed inertia and temperature dynamics (better captures thermal dynamics)."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RabbitMQServer
]

instance KalmanFilterStateEstimator : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Kalman filter implementation for heatbed temperature state estimation and anomaly detection using real-time sensor data."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RabbitMQServer
]

instance InternalTemperatureDatabase : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series database storing real-time internal temperature data for visualization and anomaly detection."
    DTDFVocab:fromData InternalTemperatureDataStream
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RabbitMQServer
]

instance ExternalTemperatureDatabase : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series database storing real-time external temperature data for environmental context analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ExternalTemperatureDataStream
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RabbitMQServer
]

instance ModelParameterCalibrationData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Automated calibration data for model parameter tracking using real-time sensor data for dynamic adjustment."
    DTDFVocab:fromData InternalTemperatureDataStream, ExternalTemperatureDataStream
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EnhancedThermalModel
]

// C11: Enablers
instance RabbitMQServer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Open-source AMQP-based message broker (RabbitMQ) for low-latency real-time data exchange between physical and digital components."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeDataStreamingService, CoSimulationService, AnomalyDetectionService
]

instance LowLevelDriver : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Custom abstraction layer for sensor/actuator communication with safety protocols (periodic temperature checks)."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataCollectionService, SafetyMonitoringService
]

instance TimeSeriesDatabase : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Time-series database system for storing and managing real-time temperature data for visualization and analysis."
    DTDFVocab:enables HistoricalDataAnalysisService
]

instance KalmanFilterImplementation : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "State estimation algorithm implementation for heatbed temperature tracking and anomaly detection using sensor data."
    DTDFVocab:enables StateEstimationService, AnomalyDetectionService
]

instance BangBangController : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Raspberry Pi-based bang-bang controller with statechart implementation for temperature regulation (parameters: LL, UL, H, C)."
    DTDFVocab:enables TemperatureRegulationService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeDataStreamingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous data streaming service for temperature sensor data (internal/external) via RabbitMQ for real-time monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:provides TemperatureVisualizationInsight
]

instance CoSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Co-simulation service integrating real-time and historical data for 'what-if' scenario analysis and controller parameter optimization."
    DTDFVocab:provides ControllerParameterOptimizationAction
]

instance AnomalyDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Anomaly detection service using Kalman filter to identify thermal inertia changes and system deviations from normal operation."
    DTDFVocab:provides AnomalyTriggeredAction
]

instance HistoricalDataAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Historical data analysis service for model validation and performance comparison against real-world data."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelValidationInsight
]

instance SafetyMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Safety monitoring service ensuring temperature limits (LL, UL) are maintained via low-level driver safety protocols."
    DTDFVocab:provides SafetyConstraintEnforcementAction
]

instance ModelCalibrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automated calibration service dynamically updating model parameters using real-time sensor data for improved accuracy."
    DTDFVocab:provides ParameterAdjustmentAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance TemperatureVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time temperature profile visualization comparing model predictions (Model B) with actual sensor readings for monitoring."
]

instance ControllerParameterOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Optimization action evaluating controller parameters (LL, UL, H, C) through co-simulation to minimize temperature deviations."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance AnomalyTriggeredAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic action triggered by Kalman filter anomalies to initiate self-adaptation workflow (data collection, model recalibration)."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ParameterAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamic parameter adjustment action for model calibration using tracking simulations to handle parameter drift."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance SafetyConstraintEnforcementAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Enforcement action to prevent unsafe states by adjusting controller parameters when temperature limits are violated."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ModelValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Performance validation insight comparing model predictions (Model B) against real-world temperature data for accuracy assessment."
]

// C1: Environment
instance IncubatorEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains HeatbedActuator, FanActuator, InternalTemperatureSensor1, InternalTemperatureSensor2, ExternalTemperatureSensor
]

instance DigitalTwinEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains SimplifiedThermalModel, EnhancedThermalModel, KalmanFilterStateEstimator, RabbitMQServer, LowLevelDriver
]


instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
  **Control Command Details:**
  - **Command Types**: Heating (heatbed) and fan control commands.
  - **Message Format**: Sent via RabbitMQ messages from the controller to the low-level driver.
  - **Control Logic**: Bang-bang control strategy (on/off heating until target temperature is reached).
  - **Feedback Mechanism**:
    - Controller receives temperature measurements from sensors via RabbitMQ.
    - Controller issues commands (heating/fan) based on reference temperature (LL, UL, H, C parameters).
  - **Validation/Authentication**: No explicit mention of validation/authentication mechanisms in documents.
  - **Command Execution Confirmation**: Not explicitly confirmed in documents (assumed implicit via system state changes).

  **Control Parameters**:
  - **LL (Lower Limit)**: Temperature threshold below which heating is not triggered.
  - **UL (Upper Limit)**: Temperature threshold above which heating is not triggered.
  - **H (Heating Duration)**: Duration the heatbed remains on.
  - **C (Waiting Duration)**: Delay after heating to prevent overshoot.
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "
    - **Synchronization Frequency**:
      - **Temperature Data Acquisition**: Periodic reading of temperature data from sensors (no explicit frequency mentioned, but implied to be **low-level, real-time** due to context of control loop).
      - **Controller Actuation Latency**: The controller waits for a **waiting duration (C)** after each actuation to ensure temperature stability (no explicit value, but inferred to be in **milliseconds to seconds** range based on typical bang-bang control systems).
      - **Calibration Process**: 'On-demand' calibration, implying **triggered by anomalies or manual intervention** rather than fixed intervals.

    - **Temporal Granularity**:
      - **Simulation/Analysis**: Models (e.g., Model A/B) are used for **real-time and offline** temperature predictions, with granularity tied to the **time-dependent models** (e.g., energy flow calculations in seconds/minutes).
      - **State Estimation**: Kalman filter operates in **real-time** (sub-second latency implied for state estimation updates).
    "
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "
    - **Architecture Pattern**:
      - **Cyber-Physical System (CPS) Model**: The DT integrates **cyber (software components)** and **physical (incubator hardware)** elements, as depicted in Figure 1 (C = Cyber, P = Physical).
      - **Component Integration**:
        - **Data Flow**: Physical Twin (PT) → Low-Level Driver → RabbitMQ → Controller/DT Components.
        - **Key Subsystems**:
          - **Models (Model A/B)**: Dynamic temperature prediction models (derived from energy flow equations).
          - **Algorithms**:
            - **Kalman Filter**: State estimation for heatbed temperature (non-measured variable).
            - **Calibration Algorithms**: On-demand model updates using historical data.
            - **Controller Logic**: Bang-bang control (statechart in Figure 8) with parameters (LL, UL, H, C).
          - **Monitoring**: Anomaly detection via Kalman filter triggers reconfiguration loops.
      - **Orchestration**:
        - **Self-Adaptation Loop**: Implemented as an 8-step process (Figure 5) for dynamic controller reconfiguration (e.g., after introducing new objects like cold water).
        - **Resource Management**: RabbitMQ handles message synchronization; no explicit mention of distributed resource allocation (assumed centralized for simplicity).
    "
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
    **Development and Deployment Phases:**
    - **Conceptual Modeling Phase:**
      - Creation of abstract models (e.g., CAD-like representations for physical components like styrofoam box, heatbed, and sensors) to understand static properties (e.g., dimensions, shapes).
      - Differentiation between static (e.g., CAD) and dynamic (e.g., differential equation-based) models for system behavior analysis.
    - **Simulation and Calibration Phase:**
      - Development of two thermal models (Model A: 2 free parameters; Model B: 4 free parameters) for predicting incubator temperature dynamics.
      - Calibration of models using real-time sensor data (e.g., temperature measurements from inside/outside the incubator) to refine parameter estimates (e.g., thermal inertia of the heatbed).
      - **Transition:** Models are validated against experimental results (e.g., temperature profiles) before integration into the Digital Twin (DT).

    **Operational and Evolutionary Phases:**
    - **Runtime Monitoring and Adaptation:**
      - Real-time visualization of incubator status via time-series databases and dashboards (e.g., listening to controller messages via RabbitMQ).
      - **Anomaly Detection:** Kalman filter integration to estimate unmeasured heatbed temperature and detect deviations from expected behavior (e.g., thermal inertia changes due to new objects).
      - **Self-Adaptation Loop:**
        - **Detection:** Anomalies trigger a self-adaptation workflow (e.g., cooling/heating experiments to gather new data).
        - **Data Collection:** Gathering data during controlled experiments (e.g., cooling to safe temperature, then heating).
        - **Model Refinement:** Recalibration of Model B with new parameters via automated calibration processes.
        - **Controller Optimization:** Running 'what-if' simulations (co-simulations with historical/real-time data) to optimize controller parameters (e.g., LL, UL, H, C).
        - **Reconfiguration:** Dynamic updates to the Kalman filter and controller based on new data.
      - **Lifecycle Governance:** Open-source hardware/software availability (GitHub repository) enables community-driven evolution, testing, and validation of DT techniques.

    **Transition Management:**
    - **Model Versioning:** Implicitly supported by open-source repository (e.g., [GitHub](https://github.com/INTO-CPS-Association/example-incubator)) for tracking model iterations (e.g., Model A → Model B).
    - **Dynamic Integration:** Models are continuously updated via calibration algorithms (e.g., real-time data-driven parameter tuning) to maintain alignment with the Physical Twin (PT).
  "
]

    base:desc "
    **Industry Standards and Frameworks:**
    - **Not Explicitly Mentioned:** No direct references to formal standards (e.g., IEEE, ISO) for Digital Twin or CPS.
    - **Technical Compliance:**
      - **Cyber-Physical Systems (CPS):** Aligns with Lee’s definition (2008) of CPS integration (computation + physical processes).
      - **Industry 4.0:** Implicitly supports reconfigurability and robustness (e.g., self-adaptation to new objects).
      - **Models@Runtime:** Co-simulation and dynamic model updates align with runtime model execution principles.
      - **Autonomic Computing:** Self-adaptation loop (steps 1–8) resembles autonomic behaviors (e.g., self-healing, self-optimization).
    - **Interoperability Standards:**

    - **Certification Requirements:**

  "
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The incubator case study includes the following **security and safety-related elements** based on the documents:

  1. **Monitoring and Safety Checks**:
     - The Digital Twin (DT) employs **calibration algorithms** to update models of the Cyber-Physical System (CPS) and environment with real-time data from sensors (e.g., temperature sensors inside/outside the styrofoam box).
     - **Monitors** are used to validate system performance for **safety and optimality**, triggering interventions (human or algorithmic) when violations occur.

  2. **Technologies for Safety-Critical Operations**:
     - The DT integrates **simulation models** (e.g., differential equations for thermal dynamics) to predict behavior under varying conditions, enabling **predictive safety analysis**.
     - The case study references **formally verified FMI-enabled data brokering** (RabbitMQ FMU) in related works ([17](https://doi.org/...)), though this is not directly applied in the incubator DT itself. The incubator DT relies on **adaptive control** (via Raspberry Pi) to regulate temperature dynamically, ensuring robustness to environmental changes.

  3. **Hardware-Software Integration**:
     - The **insulated styrofoam box** and **heatbed/fan setup** form a **closed-loop CPS** where physical components (sensors, actuators) are interfaced via a **Raspberry Pi controller**, ensuring real-time feedback for safety-critical adjustments.
     - **No explicit mention** of cybersecurity protocols (e.g., encryption, access controls) or fail-safe mechanisms beyond the adaptive control logic is provided in the documents.

  **Key Limitations**:
     - The documents do not detail **risk assessment methodologies**, **threat mitigation strategies**, or **compliance with safety standards** (e.g., ISO 26262 for automotive-grade systems). The focus is on **operational safety** via simulation and real-time monitoring, not formal security frameworks."
]
